# New Epics to Implement

## Still not retrieving distribution history for new symbols

It seems that when we add a new symbol to the universe, either during the CSV import or using the add button (+) on the universe screen, we correctly get the current distribution, but we do not get enough of the distribution history to determine if the distributions are annual, quarterly, monthly, or weekly. This is a problem because we need to know the distribution frequency to annualize the yield for the symbol correctly.

This is a critical issue because without the distribution history, we cannot accurately calculate the annualized yield for the symbol, which is a key metric for evaluating the performance of the investment. We need to ensure that when a new symbol is added, we retrieve enough of the distribution history to determine the frequency of distributions and calculate the annualized yield correctly.

You attempted to address this issue in Epic 58: "New Symbol Distribution Cycle", but it seems like the issue is still not resolved. Investigate why we are not retrieving enough of the distribution history for new symbols and implement a solution to fix this issue once and for all.

Don't forget to make sure you can reproduce the issue prior to fixing the problem. Use the Playwright MCP server to verify that you can replicate the issue and that you've fixed it correctly.

Don't forget to add test coverage to ensure that this issue does not regress in the future. We need to make sure that when a new symbol is added, we are correctly retrieving the distribution history and calculating the annualized yield based on the distribution frequency.

This issue is holding up the release of the product, so it should be a high priority to fix.

## Still not dealing with splits

I see the message "No open lots for ${symbol}$..."

The data in the CSV is reverse date order grouped by account. You SHOULD be processing the data in reverse order, they you would have the open lots you are looking for when you get to dealing with the splits.

You've tried to address this issue in the following epics:

- Epic 48: CSV Import Split Handling
- Epic 57: Fix CSV Reverse-Split Import Handling
- Epic 61: Stock Splits — CUSIP-Symbol Lot Adjustment

I think we may be close now that I see this error message. The fact that you are seeing the message "No open lots for ${symbol}$..." means that you are not correctly processing the CSV in reverse order, because if you were, you would have the open lots you are looking for when you get to dealing with the splits.

This is also a critical issue that is holding up release, so it should be a high priority to fix. Please investigate why we are not processing the CSV in reverse order and implement a solution to fix this issue once and for all.

Use the Playwright MCP server to verify that you can replicate the issue and that you've fixed it correctly. Don't forget to add test coverage to ensure that this issue does not regress in the future. We need to make sure that we are correctly processing the CSV in reverse order so that we have the open lots we need when we get to dealing with the splits.

## Still seeing unloaded symbols when I scroll down

Where before I was seeing unloaded symbols at the beginning of the sorted list. Now I am seeing them at the end of the sorted list when I scroll down. But I don't see it right away. I have to scroll down and lazy load data a few times before I see the unloaded symbols. I still believe the problem is with signals not processing correctly.

Don't forget to reproduce the problem using the Playwright MCP server before you attempt to fix it. Once you have a reliable way to replicate the issue, investigate why we are still seeing unloaded symbols when scrolling down and implement a solution to fix this issue once and for all. This is a critical issue that impacts the usability of the universe screen, so it should be a high priority to fix. Don't forget to add test coverage to ensure that this issue does not regress in the future. We need to make sure that all symbols are loaded correctly as users scroll through their investments, so that they can see all of their holdings without any missing data.

## Determine if new Symbol is a CEF

Currently, when we add a new symbol to the universe, we assume it is an equity. But since we are adding symbols now that are from closed end funds that could be classified as Income, we need to first determine if the new symbol is part of the Closed End Fund universe, and if it is, we need to classify it as either Equity, Income, or Tax Free.

You can do this by looking up the stock on CefConnect.com like we do elsewhere in the application. Find the stock and then classify it like we do when we run the screener.

There should be enough existing code for you to figure out how to implement this new feature and you could probably reuse a lot of the existing code by making them functions if they are not already functions so you don't have to do much more than refactor existing code and then call it as part of adding a new symbol to the universe.
